touch base with you guys again, because I think you might be a really powerful way for us to hit those goals in our coming year.

So overall, we're at a point where we're kind of planning out our year of 26 and what we're actually going to need to accomplish.
And what we're seeing across the board is that I think the messaging that we're putting out to our prospects, to our folks on the lifecycle marketing side is just not exactly right.
It is.

It's just not clear enough.
It's not strong enough.

It's like people staying up late at night trying to figure out, oh, how do I take these complex manufacturing concepts and articulate that in a clean and interesting way back to the experts?
It's an interesting challenge.
And so I could see us using or basically the hole that we need to fill in terms of content is probably first and foremost, lifecycle marketing.
There's so many interesting things that we do that we just don't have an arm or a muscle to talk about.
The second is, you know, sort of upgrading our materials.
So a really clear example of that is our website, which is, I think I wrote like three years ago at midnight.
Like it really does not express what we do as a business.
And so we're actively losing leads because our content on our website is just out of date and not interesting.
The third and probably order of priority is the materials that our sales team uses.
So we know why we're different than competitor X, Y, Z.
We don't have battle cards.
We don't have strong decks.
We really could, and we just, we don't have that sort of, again, content arm to get ourselves there.
And I think the only spot that I wouldn't want to outsource at this point, the only thing that really does seem to be working is we're working with a different agency to create our LinkedIn posts.
And that seems to be going really well.
So I probably just would not touch that.
Or if anything, we'd want to create a symbiotic, you know, experience between that agency and, and whatever we bring in here.
Varun, anything you would add to that list?

SEO, GEO sit on top, et cetera, et cetera.

And like, you know, think about scaling investment in different channels.
It actually, I think is a great idea to reevaluate how we think about the foundation because like, you know, you put garbage into performance marketing ads and you don't have a great website, right?
You're going to be like, oh, this channel is not converting, but maybe it's the foundation that we need to reevaluate.

And Tyler, think you went, you guys anchor more on the SEO and GEO side, but like within that, like landscape of content underlaying these different channels, you guys also work with your clients to take that content and repurpose it across the board.
Yeah, absolutely.
It typically comes later in the partnership just because if you haven't created a lot of content on your marketing site, that's the biggest need.
Like your domain authority is going to be lower.
The amount of different questions that your ICP might have where you could be a citation or you could be in the answer, you just don't have enough shots on goal, right?
So like what our approach is, is our first metric we want to look at is just driving higher intent organic traffic.
And then we want to follow that through to the conversion of that.
And so, you know, once we've created a lot of content, then we can start thinking about, well, how do we repurpose that for social?
How do we repurpose that to be like a webinar that you did, right?

would say the gap is simpler, which is we don't have anyone doing a lot of content internally right now.

And so, you know, our team is basically going in and executing this content at scale for our partners.
Okay.
Interesting.

in in background, in the background,
